# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our application deployment without resource limits or requests. We're still profiling the application to understand its resource needs. Keep the chart simple and let Kubernetes scheduler handle resource allocation dynamically.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: 
 name: helm-chart-sa
---  
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:   
name: app-configmap     # Name of the config map to use.
data:        
appName : "myApp"       # Application's name, replace with your application’s actual name 
imagePullSecrets: []      # If you have private images in quay or dockerhub then provide a secret for pulling those image  
envVars: |   
  - key=MY_VAR1     # Environment variable keys and values. Replace MY_APP with your application's actual name, replace the value as per requirement      
image : myAppImage      # Docker Image Name to use in deployment file (replace 'myapp')  
replicaCount: 3          # Number of replicas for pods   
resources: {}             # Resources requested by container. Default is CPU and memory set at 10% each if not specified here    
nodeSelector :{}           # Node selector terms to select the nodes on which this deployment should run  
tolerations: []            # Toleration labels for pods in a node, default no toleration added   
affinity: {}              # Affinities rules applied when scheduling Pods. Default is none if not specified here 
serviceAccountName : helm-chart-sa     # Name of the service account to use  
annotations:{}            # Annotation for Helm chart, like version and description   
version: "1"              # Version number (default set as 'v0')     
licenseType: Apache2       # License type. Default is not specified if it's a custom application
